data.title: Hershey suitors have been rebuffed, hard to buy chocolate maker - analyst
ONE Gas issues upside 2025 EPS guidance, five-year financial growth rate targets
data.description:
ONE Gas (NYSE:OGS) said Wednesday it forecasts FY 2025 net income of $254M-$261M and earnings per share of $4.20-$4.32, mostly above the $4.20 analyst consensus estimate.

The company expects FY 2025 capital spending of ~$750M, primarily targeted for system integrity and replacement projects, with an anticipated average rate base of $5.8B.

For the five years ending in 2029, ONE Gas (OGS) guided for average annual capital spending in the $750M-$850M range, or ~$4B for the total five-year period, including ~$1B in growth capital.

Capital expenditures support estimated average annual rate base growth of 7%-9% through 2029, the company said.

ONE Gas (OGS) also guided for annual net income and EPS growth averaging 7%-9% and 4%-6% respectively over the long term, which it expects will come in at the high end of the respective ranges through 2029.

Consistent with last year's guidance, ONE Gas (OGS) expects to achieve a 1%-2% average annual dividend growth rate through 2029, with a target dividend payout ratio of 55%-65% of net income.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Synopsys expects double-digit rev growth in 2025; beats consensus in FQ4
Academy Sports + Outdoors announces new $700M share buyback program and quarterly cash dividend
data.description:
• Academy Sports and Outdoors (NASDAQ:ASO) board approved a new share repurchase program to repurchase up to $700M of its outstanding common stock.
  • The new share repurchase program replaces the preceding share repurchase program, of which $423M remained at the time this new program was approved, and is effective as of December 4, 2024, for a period of three years.
  • The board also approved the declaration of a quarterly cash dividend with respect to the fiscal quarter ended November 2, 2024, of $0.11 per share of the company's common stock.
  • The dividend is payable on January 15, 2025, to stockholders of record as of the close of business on December 18, 2024.
  • ASO shares were up 5.5% in after hours trading on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AES announces pricing of $500M notes in public offering
data.description:
• AES (NYSE:AES) announced, on Wednesday, the pricing of $500M aggregate principal amount of its 6.950% fixed-to-fixed reset rate junior subordinated notes due 2055.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur, subject to certain customary closing conditions, on December 6, 2024.
  • AES intends to use the net proceeds from the proposed offering to repay existing indebtedness, including borrowings under the revolving facility of its senior credit facility, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: IDT Corporation Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71, revenue of $309.5M
data.description:
• IDT Corporation press release (NYSE:IDT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71.
  • Revenue of $309.5M (+2.8% Y/Y).

  • Consolidated results for all periods presented include corporate overhead. In 1Q25, Corporate G&A expense was $2.9 million compared to $2.8 million in 1Q24.

  • As of October 31, 2024, IDT held $180.4 million in cash, cash equivalents, debt securities, and current equity investments, a decrease from $193.0 million at July 31, 2024. The decrease predominantly reflects the timing of payments made by IDT to cover anticipated BOSS Money disbursement prefunding.

  • Also at July 31, 2024, current assets totaled $431.7 million and current liabilities totaled $269.8 million. The Company had no outstanding debt at the quarter end.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities decreased to $0.2 million in 1Q25 from $14.9 million in 1Q24. Exclusive of changes in customer funds deposits at IDT’s Fintech segment, net cash provided by operating activities was negative $(2.6) million in 1Q25 and positive $17.9 million in 1Q24. Capital expenditures increased to $5.3 million in 1Q25 from $4.3 million in 1Q24.

data.title: PVH Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.44, revenue of $2.25B beats by $30M
data.description:
• PVH press release (NYSE:PVH): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.03 beats by $0.44.
  • Revenue of $2.25B (-4.7% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Full year outlook
  • Revenue: Reaffirms projected decrease of 6% to 7% (decrease 6% to 7% on a constant currency basis)
  • Operating margin:
  • GAAP basis: Projected to be approximately 9.2% compared to approximately 9.8% previously
  • Non-GAAP basis: Reaffirms outlook of approximately flat compared to 10.1% in 2023
  • EPS:
  • GAAP basis: Projected to be in a range of $10.55 to $10.70 compared to $11.20 to $11.45 previously
  • Non-GAAP basis: Projected to be in a range of $11.55 to $11.70 compared to $11.55 to $11.80 previously
  • Updated EPS guidance includes negative impact of approximately $0.15 per share related to foreign currency translation compared to approximately $0.05 previously

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: SentinelOne raises FY rev guidance; beats on topline in FQ3
Petrobras chairman set to depart for position at Brazil oil regulator - report
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) -0.9% in Wednesday's trading following a report that Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva will appoint Pietro Mendes, the company's chairman, to a senior position at oil regulator ANP.

The government will appoint current board member Bruno Moretti to replace Mendes as chairman, according to the report from local newspaper O Globo.

Separately, Brazil prosecutors have asked Petrobras (PBR) to conduct more studies before drilling off the coast of the Amazon rain forest, citing potential effects of extreme weather on tides, according to documents filed by the Ibama environmental agency.

The company wants to explore for oil fields off the far northern coast of Brazil in the Equatorial Margin, which is considered the country's most promising oil frontier due to shared geology with nearby Guyana.

data.title: Adeia and Sharp renew multi-year IP license agreement
data.description:
• Adeia (NASDAQ:ADEA) announced on Wednesday that Sharp Corporation, a global smart TV and consumer electronics manufacturer, has agreed to renew a multi-year worldwide license for Adeia's media intellectual property portfolio.
  • This multi-year agreement extends the longstanding relationship between the two companies, encompassing Adeia's media IP portfolio.
